behavior_summary: When the player examines (looks at) the LEGGING item, the script displays a single short description/bark: "leggings ".
After emitting the bark the process yields/suspends, ending the U8P_LEGGING::look handler. No branching, menus, or external calls are performed.

The item is annotated with an armourClass = 3 property in the surrounding metadata (inferred to mean these are wearable leg armour), but this script only provides the U8P_LEGGING::look description and does not modify item state.
notable_control_flow: Entry: invoked by the engine's U8P_LEGGING::look/examine process for the item (process form).
Action: issue a single Bark/dialogue line containing the short description.
Exit: suspend/yield immediately after the bark; no further logic or branching.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the bark text "leggings " intentional or an artifact of extraction?
Is the armourClass = 3 used elsewhere by other scripts or systems (this script does not reference it)?
Does the suspend node represent a simple end-of-process or does it hand control back to a caller that expects additional behavior?

main_function.name: U8P_LEGGING::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description shown to the player when they examine the LEGGING item; present a single bark line and then suspend the U8P_LEGGING::look process.
entry_conditions: Player invokes the engine 'U8P_LEGGING::look' (examine) action on the LEGGING object (processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_LEGGING::look
function_map.role: Handle the object's U8P_LEGGING::look/examine event and display its short description.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the player examines (looks at) the LEGGING item, the script displays a single short description/bark: "leggings ".
